segments.text: Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I sink in deep mire, where there is no standing; I have come into deep waters, where the floods overwhelm me.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: deep mire: Heb. the mire of depth
deep waters: Heb. depth of waters

segments.text: I am weary with my crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes grow dim with waiting for my God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: More in number than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause; mighty are those who would destroy me, those who attack me with lies. What I did not steal must I now restore?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O God, you know my folly; the wrongs I have done are not hidden from you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let not those who hope in you be put to shame through me, O Lord God of hosts; let not those who seek you be dishonored through me, O God of Israel.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For it is for your sake that I have borne reproach, that dishonor has covered my face.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother's sons.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For zeal for your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When I wept and humbled my soul with fasting, it became my reproach.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Those who sit in the gate speak against me, and the drunkards make songs about me.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: drunkards: Heb. drinkers of strong drink

segments.text: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O Lord. At an acceptable time, O God, in the abundance of your steadfast love answer me in your saving faithfulness.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Deliver me from sinking in the mire; let me be delivered from my enemies and from the deep waters.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let not the flood sweep me away, or the deep swallow me up, or the pit close its mouth over me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Answer me, O Lord, for your steadfast love is good; according to your abundant mercy, turn to me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Hide not your face from your servant, for I am in distress; make haste to answer me.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: hear…: Heb. make haste to hear me

segments.text: Draw near to my soul, redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You know my reproach, and my shame and my dishonor; my foes are all known to you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Reproaches have broken my heart, so that I am in despair. I looked for pity, but there was none, and for comforters, but I found none.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: to take…: Heb. to lament with me

segments.text: They gave me poison for food, and for my thirst they gave me sour wine to drink.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their own table before them become a snare; let it be a trap for their safety.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they cannot see, and make their loins tremble continually.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Pour out your indignation upon them, and let your burning anger overtake them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May their camp be a desolation; let no one dwell in their tents.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: let none…: Heb. let there not be a dweller

segments.text: For they persecute him whom you have struck down, and they recount the pain of those you have wounded.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Add to them punishment upon punishment; may they have no acquittal from you.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: iniquity unto…: or, punishment of iniquity, etc

segments.text: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living; let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But I am afflicted and in pain; let your salvation, O God, set me on high!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will praise the name of God with a song; I will magnify him with thanksgiving.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: This will please the Lord more than an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When the humble see it they will be glad; you who seek God, let your hearts revive.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For the Lord hears the needy and does not despise his own people who are prisoners.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let heaven and earth praise him, the seas and everything that moves in them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For God will save Zion and build up the cities of Judah, and people shall dwell there and possess it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The offspring of his servants shall inherit it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.
segments.speaker: David
